basic_details.job_title: Indian Air Force (IAF) Intake 02/2027 Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for Agniveervayu & Airmen Group Y Posts
basic_details.short_information: Both notifications share the same recruiting body, application portal, and exam window, but differ in eligibility, tenure, and pay structure. Here's a quick snapshot of both:

application_fee.details: Both notifications carry the same non-refundable examination fee, payable online after submitting the application form. | Payment can be made via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. The fee is non-refundable under any circumstances, including for duplicate or rejected applications.

qualification.details: This trade has two qualification routes - one for 10+2 candidates and another for Diploma/Pharmacy graduates - each with a different age bracket. | 10+2 Route: Passed 10+2/Intermediate with Physics, Chemistry, Biology & English with minimum 50% aggregate and 50% in English (or equivalent 2-year Vocational Course with the same non-vocational subjects). Diploma/Pharmacy Route: Passed 10+2/Intermediate with PCB & English (50% aggregate, 50% English) PLUS a Diploma/B.Pharma/B.Sc in Pharmacy with minimum 50% marks, along with valid registration from the State Pharmacy Council or Pharmacy Council of India (PCI) at the time of enrolment.

salary_details.details: Pay structures differ significantly between the two postings, reflecting their different service models - Agniveervayu under the Agnipath Scheme versus a regular 20-year Airmen cadre. | On completion of four years, Agniveervayu receive a one-time Seva Nidhi package of approximately ₹10.04 lakh (excluding interest), along with a non-contributory life insurance cover of ₹48 lakh during the engagement period. They are not entitled to gratuity, pension, or Provident Fund contributions, and do not receive Ex-Servicemen (ESM) status after discharge. Up to 25% of each batch may be offered the opportunity to apply for the regular Airmen cadre based on performance. | Training Stipend: ₹14,600 per month during training. Post-Training Salary: Starting gross emoluments of minimum ₹26,900 per month (including Military Service Pay), plus applicable Dearness Allowance. Other Allowances: Transport Allowance, CPMA, Leave Ration Allowance, Children Education Allowance, HRA, and more. Leave: 60 days annual leave + 30 days casual leave per calendar year. Insurance: Group Insurance cover of ₹62.5 lakh at a premium of ₹3,200/month. Job Role: Training in nursing and first-aid, management of medical stores/dispensaries, and ward supervision duties.

selection_process: Agniveervayu (Other than Science) 45 minutes English, Reasoning & General Awareness
Agniveervayu (Both Streams) 85 minutes Physics, Mathematics, English, RAGA
Airmen Medical Assistant 45 minutes English, Reasoning & General Awareness
Both posts follow a three-phase selection process - an online written test
Marking Scheme (both posts): +1 mark for each correct answer
and -0.25 for each wrong answer. Marks may be normalised to adjust for variation in difficulty across exam sessions
and candidates must qualify in each subject/paper individually.

Shortlisted candidates undergo the Physical Fitness Test (detailed above) followed by two written Adaptability Tests assessing suitability for military life and ability to adjust to Air Force working conditions and environments.
Candidates who clear Adaptability Test-II undergo a detailed medical examination by the Air Force Medical Team
and ECG. Candidates declared unfit may appeal via an Appeal Medical Board by depositing ₹40.

physical_eligibility.minimum height (female): 152 cms (147 cms for NE/hilly Uttarakhand; 150 cms for Lakshadweep)
physical_eligibility.details: Both posts require candidates to meet height, chest, hearing, dental, and visual standards, with separate visual acuity criteria for each trade. | Chest (Male): Minimum 77 cms with at least 5 cms expansion. Chest (Female - Agniveervayu only): Minimum 5 cms expansion. Hearing: Able to hear forced whisper from 6 metres, each ear separately. Dental: Healthy gums and minimum 14 dental points; orthodontic appliances and dental implants are disqualifying. LASIK/PRK history: Not accepted for either post. Tattoos: Permanent tattoos are restricted to the inner forearm and back of the hand only; offensive tattoos are disqualifying regardless of location. | Candidates who clear the online test must pass a 1.6 km run followed by push-ups, sit-ups, and squats within a defined time limit.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted entirely online in two steps through the CASB portal.
Step I (Registration): Enter personal and contact details, then click "Generate OTP." Verify both mobile number and email ID using the OTPs received.
Agree to the terms & conditions, re-verify entered details, and click "Submit" to receive your User ID and Password via email/SMS.
Step II (Application Form): Log in and click "Go To Application" to fill in personal, communication, and qualification details.
Upload scanned photograph, signature, parent's signature (if under 18), and left thumb impression as per specified size/format guidelines.
Capture a live photo via webcam for face-match verification.
Preview the form, correct any errors, and click "Submit."
Pay the examination fee of ₹550 + 18% GST through the payment gateway.
Print and retain a copy of the final submitted application form for future reference.
Candidates must indicate five preferred examination city choices while filling the application. Once submitted, the application cannot be withdrawn, and the fee is non-refundable.
important_instructions: Mobile phones, smartwatches, and electronic devices are strictly prohibited in testing areas during Phase-I and Phase-II; violators face permanent debarment. No TA/DA is admissible for travel, certificate verification, or medical examination at any stage. Requests for change of exam centre, date, or medical examination centre will not be entertained. Inclusion in the Provisional Select List (PSL) does not guarantee enrolment; it remains valid for six months from the date of display. Selection is fair and merit-based - no bribe or intermediary is required at any stage. Candidates should beware of impersonators and unauthorized agents. Corrigenda or updates will be published only on the official CASB portal, not through newspapers or other media.

content_sections.heading: IAF Recruitment 2026 - Overview
content_sections.text: Both notifications share the same recruiting body, application portal, and exam window, but differ in eligibility, tenure, and pay structure. Here's a quick snapshot of both:

content_sections.heading: IAF Agniveervayu Intake 02/2027 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Agniveervayu eligibility is divided across age, marital status, and educational qualification requirements specific to Science and Non-Science streams. Candidates born between 01 July 2005 and 01 January 2010 (both dates inclusive) are eligible to apply. If a candidate clears all stages of selection, the upper age limit on the date of enrolment must not exceed 22 years. Only unmarried male and female candidates are eligible. Candidates must undertake not to marry during the four-year engagement period; female candidates must additionally undertake not to become pregnant during this period, as pregnancy or marriage during service can lead to dismissal. Candidates eligible under Science Subjects may also opt to appear for both Science and Other-than-Science papers in a single sitting while registering. Candidates must select one domicile category at the time of registration, which cannot be changed later, as shortlisting and the Provisional Select List are prepared state-wise: Permanent Domicile: Permanent residents of districts of the State/UT, supported by a Domicile Certificate from a Gazetted Revenue Officer. COAFP-I: Children of currently serving Air Force personnel posted in the relevant State/UT. COAFP-II: Children of retired/discharged/deceased Air Force personnel residing in the State/UT for a minimum of one year.

rows.qualification_required: 10+2/Intermediate with Maths, Physics & English (50% aggregate + 50% in English) OR 3-year Diploma in Engineering (Mechanical/Electrical/Electronics/Automobile/Computer Science/Instrumentation/IT) with 50% marks OR 2-year Vocational Course with Physics & Maths (50% marks)
10+2/Intermediate in any stream (50% aggregate + 50% in English) OR 2-year Vocational Course (50% aggregate + 50% in English)

content_sections.heading: IAF Airmen Group Y Medical Assistant 02/2027 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: This trade has two qualification routes - one for 10+2 candidates and another for Diploma/Pharmacy graduates - each with a different age bracket. 10+2 Route: Passed 10+2/Intermediate with Physics, Chemistry, Biology & English with minimum 50% aggregate and 50% in English (or equivalent 2-year Vocational Course with the same non-vocational subjects). Diploma/Pharmacy Route: Passed 10+2/Intermediate with PCB & English (50% aggregate, 50% English) PLUS a Diploma/B.Pharma/B.Sc in Pharmacy with minimum 50% marks, along with valid registration from the State Pharmacy Council or Pharmacy Council of India (PCI) at the time of enrolment.

content_sections.heading: IAF Recruitment 2026 - Physical & Medical Standards
content_sections.text: Both posts require candidates to meet height, chest, hearing, dental, and visual standards, with separate visual acuity criteria for each trade. Chest (Male): Minimum 77 cms with at least 5 cms expansion. Chest (Female - Agniveervayu only): Minimum 5 cms expansion. Hearing: Able to hear forced whisper from 6 metres, each ear separately. Dental: Healthy gums and minimum 14 dental points; orthodontic appliances and dental implants are disqualifying. LASIK/PRK history: Not accepted for either post. Tattoos: Permanent tattoos are restricted to the inner forearm and back of the hand only; offensive tattoos are disqualifying regardless of location. Candidates who clear the online test must pass a 1.6 km run followed by push-ups, sit-ups, and squats within a defined time limit.

rows.agniveervayu: 152 cms (147 cms for NE/hilly Uttarakhand; 150 cms for Lakshadweep)

content_sections.heading: IAF Recruitment 2026 - Stipend & Benefits
content_sections.text: Pay structures differ significantly between the two postings, reflecting their different service models - Agniveervayu under the Agnipath Scheme versus a regular 20-year Airmen cadre. On completion of four years, Agniveervayu receive a one-time Seva Nidhi package of approximately ₹10.04 lakh (excluding interest), along with a non-contributory life insurance cover of ₹48 lakh during the engagement period. They are not entitled to gratuity, pension, or Provident Fund contributions, and do not receive Ex-Servicemen (ESM) status after discharge. Up to 25% of each batch may be offered the opportunity to apply for the regular Airmen cadre based on performance. Training Stipend: ₹14,600 per month during training. Post-Training Salary: Starting gross emoluments of minimum ₹26,900 per month (including Military Service Pay), plus applicable Dearness Allowance. Other Allowances: Transport Allowance, CPMA, Leave Ration Allowance, Children Education Allowance, HRA, and more. Leave: 60 days annual leave + 30 days casual leave per calendar year. Insurance: Group Insurance cover of ₹62.5 lakh at a premium of ₹3,200/month. Job Role: Training in nursing and first-aid, management of medical stores/dispensaries, and ward supervision duties.

content_sections.heading: IAF Selection Process 2026: Exam Pattern
content_sections.text: Both posts follow a three-phase selection process - an online written test, physical/adaptability tests, and a final medical examination. Marking Scheme (both posts): +1 mark for each correct answer, 0 for unattempted questions, and -0.25 for each wrong answer. Marks may be normalised to adjust for variation in difficulty across exam sessions, and candidates must qualify in each subject/paper individually. Shortlisted candidates undergo the Physical Fitness Test (detailed above) followed by two written Adaptability Tests assessing suitability for military life and ability to adjust to Air Force working conditions and environments. Candidates who clear Adaptability Test-II undergo a detailed medical examination by the Air Force Medical Team, including blood haemogram, urine tests, biochemistry profile (blood sugar, cholesterol, kidney & liver function), chest X-ray, and ECG. Candidates declared unfit may appeal via an Appeal Medical Board by depositing ₹40.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Both notifications carry the same non-refundable examination fee, payable online after submitting the application form. Payment can be made via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. The fee is non-refundable under any circumstances, including for duplicate or rejected applications.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for IAF Agniveervayu/Airmen Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted entirely online in two steps through the CASB portal. Visit the official portal: iafrecruitment.edcil.co.in Step I (Registration): Enter personal and contact details, then click "Generate OTP." Verify both mobile number and email ID using the OTPs received. Agree to the terms & conditions, re-verify entered details, and click "Submit" to receive your User ID and Password via email/SMS. Step II (Application Form): Log in and click "Go To Application" to fill in personal, communication, and qualification details. Upload scanned photograph, signature, parent's signature (if under 18), and left thumb impression as per specified size/format guidelines. Capture a live photo via webcam for face-match verification. Preview the form, correct any errors, and click "Submit." Pay the examination fee of ₹550 + 18% GST through the payment gateway. Print and retain a copy of the final submitted application form for future reference. Candidates must indicate five preferred examination city choices while filling the application. Once submitted, the application cannot be withdrawn, and the fee is non-refundable. Direct Link to Apply

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for Phase II Testing
content_sections.text: Shortlisted candidates must carry original documents along with self-attested photocopies for verification at the Airmen Selection Centre (ASC): Colour printout of Phase-II Admit Card and the filled application form Eight passport-size colour photographs (same as used in registration) Matriculation passing certificate and marksheet 10+2/Intermediate/Diploma/Vocational passing certificate and marksheets Diploma/B.Pharma/B.Sc Pharmacy certificate (Medical Assistant Diploma route only) Domicile/COAFP Certificate (Agniveervayu) or SOAFP Certificate, if applicable (Medical Assistant) Original Phase-I Admit Card bearing Air Force seal and invigilator's signature NCC 'A'/'B'/'C' Certificate, if applicable HB pencil, eraser, sharpener, glue stick, stapler, and a black/blue ball pen DigiLocker-verified certificates are accepted, provided candidates carry the printout along with an accessible mobile device for verification.

content_sections.heading: Important Points to Note
content_sections.text: Mobile phones, smartwatches, and electronic devices are strictly prohibited in testing areas during Phase-I and Phase-II; violators face permanent debarment. No TA/DA is admissible for travel, certificate verification, or medical examination at any stage. Requests for change of exam centre, date, or medical examination centre will not be entertained. Inclusion in the Provisional Select List (PSL) does not guarantee enrolment; it remains valid for six months from the date of display. Selection is fair and merit-based - no bribe or intermediary is required at any stage. Candidates should beware of impersonators and unauthorized agents. Corrigenda or updates will be published only on the official CASB portal, not through newspapers or other media.

faq.question: Q1. When will the IAF Agniveervayu 2026 online examination be conducted?
faq.answer: The Indian Air Force will conduct the online examination on 22 and 23 September 2026 for both recruitment notifications.

faq.question: Q2. What is the application fee for IAF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must pay a non-refundable application fee of ₹550 plus 18% GST through online payment modes such as UPI, debit card, credit card, or net banking.

faq.question: Q3. Who can apply for IAF Agniveervayu Intake 02/2027?
faq.answer: Unmarried male and female Indian citizens born between 01 July 2005 and 01 January 2010 and meeting the educational eligibility criteria can apply for Agniveervayu Intake 02/2027.

faq.question: Q4. Who is eligible for Airmen Group Y Medical Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only male Indian citizens and Gorkha candidates from Nepal with the required PCB qualification or Pharmacy Diploma/Degree are eligible to apply for the Medical Assistant trade.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary of Agniveervayu under the Agnipath Scheme?
faq.answer: Agniveervayu recruits receive a starting monthly package of ₹30,000 in the first year, increasing annually up to ₹40,000 in the fourth year, along with Seva Nidhi benefits after completion of service.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for IAF Agniveervayu and Medical Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of Phase-I Online Test, Phase-II Physical Fitness Test and Adaptability Tests, and Phase-III Medical Examination.

faq.question: Q8. What are the educational qualifications required for IAF Agniveervayu Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed 10+2 with required subjects and minimum marks, or possess an eligible Engineering Diploma or Vocational Course qualification as specified in the official notification.

faq.question: Q9. How can I apply online for IAF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through the official CASB recruitment portal by completing registration, filling the application form, uploading documents, paying the fee, and submitting the form before 26 July 2026.

faq.question: When will the IAF Agniveervayu 2026 online examination be conducted?
faq.answer: The Indian Air Force will conduct the online examination on 22 and 23 September 2026 for both recruitment notifications.

faq.answer: Candidates must pay a non-refundable application fee of ₹550 plus 18% GST through online payment modes such as UPI, debit card, credit card, or net banking.

faq.answer: Unmarried male and female Indian citizens born between 01 July 2005 and 01 January 2010 and meeting the educational eligibility criteria can apply for Agniveervayu Intake 02/2027.

faq.question: Who is eligible for Airmen Group Y Medical Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only male Indian citizens and Gorkha candidates from Nepal with the required PCB qualification or Pharmacy Diploma/Degree are eligible to apply for the Medical Assistant trade.

faq.answer: Agniveervayu recruits receive a starting monthly package of ₹30,000 in the first year, increasing annually up to ₹40,000 in the fourth year, along with Seva Nidhi benefits after completion of service.

faq.answer: The selection process consists of Phase-I Online Test, Phase-II Physical Fitness Test and Adaptability Tests, and Phase-III Medical Examination.
